disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established Crohn's disease on maintenance biologic therapy, with a recently MISSED biologic dose due to a supply issue
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any bowel conditions?
Are you on any regular medicines or injections?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've had Crohn's for years and I'm normally on a regular biologic injection, but I missed my last dose because the pharmacy had a supply problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sequential 24-hour onset: colicky abdominal pain first, then bilateral knee and right ankle swelling, then severe diarrhoea and immobility — a concurrent gut-and-joint flare
trigger_question_examples: Can you walk me through how this started and in what order?
Which came first, the tummy pain or the joint swelling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started yesterday morning with gripping belly pain and diarrhoea, and then by the evening both my knees and my right ankle swelled right up. Tonight I just can't move at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical large-joint involvement (both knees plus right ankle), swollen, warm and tender — the patient cannot weight-bear
trigger_question_examples: Which joints are affected and how many?
Are the joints hot or swollen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both knees and my right ankle — they're all swollen, warm and really tender, and I can't put any weight on them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Still passing watery diarrhoea and flatus; abdomen is crampy but soft, not board-hard or continuously vomiting (argues against bowel obstruction / surgical abdomen)
trigger_question_examples: Are you still passing wind and stool, or has everything stopped?
Is your tummy hard and rigid, or soft? Are you vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm still passing lots of watery diarrhoea and wind, it hasn't stopped. My tummy is crampy and a bit bloated but it's not hard like a board, and I'm not vomiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single fiery-red maximally-painful joint and no spiking fever or rigors (argues against septic arthritis despite warm effusions)
trigger_question_examples: Is any one joint far worse, bright red and unbearable to touch?
Have you had shaking chills or a high spiking fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, they're all swollen together rather than one being much worse or bright red, and I haven't had shaking chills — my temperature is only mildly up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild dehydration — dry mouth, unable to maintain safe oral intake because of the diarrhoea and immobility
trigger_question_examples: Are you managing to drink and keep fluids down?
Is your mouth dry, and are you passing normal amounts of urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My mouth is really dry and I've hardly been able to drink or keep much down, and I've not been to the toilet to pass water as much as usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Functional and social risk — lives alone, bedbound tonight, cannot reach the toilet, high fall risk, no carer present
trigger_question_examples: Is there anyone at home who can help you tonight?
Are you able to move around and get to the bathroom safely?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and there's no one here to help. I'm stuck in bed — I can't get to the toilet and I'm scared I'll fall trying.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound fatigue and over-the-counter paracetamol ineffective; no NSAID use reported
trigger_question_examples: What have you tried for the pain so far?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm exhausted, completely wiped out. I took paracetamol and it did nothing at all, and I haven't taken anything else.
